calendar.matters:
Petitioner To Determine The Validity And Invalidity Of Certain Trust And Will Instruments...
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Settlement Agreement And Authorizing Payment From Trust Estate
Off Calendar Jun-14-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Linda Soo Hoo and Gail Soo Hoo (Co-Trustees).
Edward Koplowitz for Linda Soo Hoo and Gail Soo Hoo (Beneficiaries).
David Mansour appeared by telephone for Joyce Soo Hoo (Beneficiary).

Mr. Mansour and Mr. Koplowitz stated that they have no objections to the Attorney's fees and costs. Ms. Ng stated that mediation lasted about ten and a half hours. The Petition for Approval of Attorney's Fees and Costs, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Orders To Determine Validity Of Power Of Appointment, Return Trust Property And Impose Constructive Trust, Remove Trustee For Breaches
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Off Calendar

Petitioner To Determine The Validity And Invalidity Of Certain Trust And Will Instruments...
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Continued To Jun-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Authorizing And Instructing Parties To Carry Out Its Terms
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Gail Soo Hoo and Linda Soo Hoo (Beneficiaries).
Lillian Ng for Gail Soo Hoo and Linda Soo Hoo (Trustees).
Graham Douds appeared by telephone for Joyce Soo Hoo.
Eleanor Roman for Bank of America.

The Court noted that the parties are seeking that the Court retain jurisdiction under CCP 664.6 and for approval of an accounting, that its only concern is that it can not approve an accounting that has not been examined by the Court and that they still have the issue of venue. Ms. Ng stated that this would be fine. Mr. Koplowitz stated that all of the parties have and still agree to the settlement, that the primary asset is in escrow and is expected to close in three days and cited cases in support of 664.6. The Court asked if the underlying petitions could now go off calendar. Mr. Douds stated that the settlement agreement provides that within 10-days of approval, the parties will dismiss their petitions, that he prefers not to take his petition off calendar and that one of the petitioners is not here. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement, etc. (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Orders to Determine Validity of Power of Appointment, etc. and the Petition to Determine the Validity and Invalidity of Certain Trust and Will Instruments are continued to May 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petitioner To Determine The Validity And Invalidity Of Certain Trust And Will Instruments...
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Continued To May-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders To Determine Validity Of Power Of Appointment, Return Trust Property And Impose Constructive Trust, Remove Trustee For Breaches
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Continued To May-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition For Order Establishing Blocked Accounts For Deposit Of Proceeds From Sale Of Trust Real Property On Waiver Of Notice
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Orders To Determine Validity Of Power Of Appointment, Return Trust Property And Impose Constructive Trust, Remove Trustee For Breaches
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Continued To Apr-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: continued to 04/23/19

Petitioner To Determine The Validity And Invalidity Of Certain Trust And Will Instruments...
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Continued To Apr-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders To Determine Validity Of Power Of Appointment, Return Trust Property And Impose Constructive Trust, Remove Trustee For Breaches
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Feb-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Gail Soo Hoo and Linda Soo Hoo (Beneficiaries).
Ian Greenside appeared by telephone for Susan Hoo-Hawley.
Evelina Manukyan for Bank of America.
Graham Douds for Joyce Soo Hoo.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that Ms. Ng was unable to be here today and that she agreed that the Parties would seek a referral to mediation. Mr. Douds raised the issue of venue being in San Francisco. The Court noted that actual administration of the trust is taking place outside of San Francisco and that neither of the two acting Trustees reside in San Francisco. Mr. Koplowitz stated that the dispute is about a six unit single piece of property in San Francisco and that he believes the matter qualifies for pro bono mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 15, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 20, 2019. The Petition for Orders to Determine Validity of Power of Appointment, etc. and the Petition to Determine the Validity and Invalidity of Certain Trust and Will Instruments are continued to and the Parties are to return on February 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. All parties agree that a referral to mediation will not affect in anyway determination of whether venue is proper (language was added to the mediation order to reflect this).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.
